chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ الْمُطَلَّقَةَ وَ هُوَ غَائِبٌ عَنْهَا تَعْتَدُّ مِنْ يَوْمَ طُلِّقَتْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The divorced woman, and he is absent from her, would observe the waiting period from the day she is divorced

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/189)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/189)
text: هُ قَالَ فِي الْغَائِبِ إِذَا طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ إِنَّهَا تَعْتَدُّ مِنَ الْيَوْمِ الَّذِي طَلَّقَهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Umar Bin Azina, from Zurara and Muhammad Bin Muslim, and Bureyd Bin Muawiya,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘With regards to the absentee, when he divorces his wife, she should observe the waiting period from the day he divorces her’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr, from ‘Umar ibn ‘Udhaynah, from Zurarah and Muhammad ibn Muslim and Burayd ibn Mu’awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “About the case of one who is absent if he divorces his wife abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘She must commence her ‘Iddah (waiting period) from the day he has divorced her.’”
